RT toxicity domains: Cardiotoxicity overlap, marrow suppression, skin recall
Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Heart, left ventricle, coronary arteries, skin, marrow
Fractionation-specific warning: Caution for left breast/chest wall, mediastinal and cardiac-adjacent RT
Timing-specific warning: Review cumulative anthracycline dose and cardiac risk factors
Medical Oncology discussion: Yes when heart in field or high cardiac risk
Senior RO review: Yes for mediastinal/left breast reirradiation/high heart dose
App warning / documentation text: Anthracycline exposure increases cardiac risk considerations when RT includes the heart or mediastinum.
Evidence level: Known cardiotoxicity + RT cardiac risk data + consensus
Verification status: Unverified first-pass inventory: reconcile against ARTG/PBS/eviQ before clinical release
Notes: Include brand/ARTG/PBS details after official reconciliation

RT toxicity domains: Severe radiosensitisation, mucositis, enteritis, pneumonitis, dermatitis, myelosuppression
Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Lung, oesophagus, bowel, stomach/duodenum, liver, skin, marrow

Timing-specific warning: Discuss washout; avoid close sequencing with high-dose RT when feasible
App warning / documentation text: Gemcitabine is a high-concern radiosensitising agent. Avoid concurrent RT unless protocol-based or explicitly justified after Med Onc/senior RO review.

RT toxicity domains: Dermatitis, mucositis, oesophagitis, pneumonitis/ILD for EGFR TKIs
Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Skin, oral/pharyngeal mucosa, oesophagus, lung
Fractionation-specific warning: Caution with head and neck RT, thoracic RT, large skin fields and hypofractionation
Timing-specific warning: Concurrent use may be protocol-based for cetuximab/HN but needs expected toxicity documentation
Medical Oncology discussion: Yes for concurrent non-protocol use or thoracic RT
Senior RO review: Yes for severe mucosal/skin fields or thoracic SABR
App warning / documentation text: EGFR-directed therapy may increase mucocutaneous and thoracic toxicity depending on site and timing.
Verification status: Unverified first-pass inventory: reconcile against ARTG/PBS/eviQ before clinical release
Notes: Include brand/ARTG/PBS details after official reconciliation

RT toxicity domains: Cytokine release/neurotoxicity context, cytopenias, infection risk, inflammatory toxicity
Highest-concern RT sites / OARs: Marrow, CNS, any recently irradiated inflammatory site
Fractionation-specific warning: Avoid unreviewed concurrent RT; urgent/palliative RT may be appropriate with specialist coordination
Timing-specific warning: Coordinate around CRS/ICANS risk, cytopenias and bridging therapy plans
App warning / documentation text: Cellular/bispecific therapies require specialist coordination with RT because of cytopenia and inflammatory toxicity uncertainties.
Verification status: Unverified first-pass inventory: reconcile against ARTG/PBS/eviQ before clinical release
